hints.text: Plate: long white + blue strip left (EV = green)
Bollard: **red strip wraps all the way around** (red front / white back reflector)
**Holey poles** (thin concrete, see-through holes NOT reaching the bottom) — HU/RO ones are wider & holes reach bottom
**Warning signs = yellow background + thin red border** (unique in Europe to Poland)
Pedestrian sign = **single horizontal line** (Austria/Belgium use two); chevrons white w/ red arrows
All-white lines, **double middle lines common**; B-type guardrails w/ round red reflectors
Polish: **Ł** (unique), Ń, Ż, Ą/Ę; consonant-heavy, uses **W not V**; direction signs green w/ distinct font
Flat & green, tree-lined roads; very Catholic → roadside shrines & Polish Catholic flags
Regional roads 3-digit, **first digit = region** (yellow boxes); landline area codes 2-digit, well-clustered
Place names: **-owo → north, -ów → south** (Kraków is southern), -ino/-yno → NW
South border = hilly/mountainous (Tatras); rest flat; wooden houses → east (Podlaskie)
Minority languages: German → Opole; Kashubian → north; Lithuanian → Puńsk
